content: Which one of the following sea routes is the shortest from point to point?
explanation: Among the given options, Chennai to Port Blair is the shortest sea route. Distance between Chennai to Port Blair is 794 nautical miles. Distance between Kolkata to Yangon is 975 nautical miles. Distance between Kolkata to Chennai is 890 nautical miles. Distance between Mumbai to Columbo is 943 nautical miles.

content: The SAARC Secretariat is located at
explanation: The SAARC Secretariat is located in Kathmandu, Nepal. It was established in the year 1985. It coordinates and monitors implementation of activities, prepares for and services meetings, and serves as a channel of communication between the Association and its Member States as well as other regional organisations. Member countries of SAARC are India, Bhutan, Bangladesh, Afghanistan, Maldives, Nepal, Pakistan and Sri Lanka.

content: Who among the following is the author of the book 'Pakistan Paradox : Instability and Resilience'?
explanation:
The Pakistan Paradox by Christopher Jaffrelot, a redoubtable scholar on South Asia, reveals a country grappling with multiple dichotomies, most of which can be traced to separatist ideals espoused by the country's early leaders.

G Parthasarathy, an Indian economist authored the Book 'Economic reforms and Rural Development in India'.

Imran Khan, ex-cricketer of Pakistan and currently a politician, wrote many books. One of them is 'Warrior Race : A Journey Through the Land of Tribal Pathan'. Imran Khan became 22nd Prime Minister of Pakistan.

Mamnoon Hussain is the 12th (2013-2018) President of Pakistan.

content: Which one of the following was the theme of the 'International Day of United Nations Peacekeepers' for the year 2015?

options.content: UN70 and UN Peacekeeping : Past, Present and Future

explanation: The theme of International Day of UN Peacekeepers, 2015, was 'UN70 and UN Peacekeeping: Past, Present and Future.' UN Peacekeepers day is celebrated on 29th May each year. It reaffirms our commitment to working together for peace. Since, its inception in 1948, the UN has established 69 peacekeeping operations in Africa, Asia, Europe and Middle East.

content: Indian athlete Vikas Gowda is associated with
explanation: Vikas Gowda is an Indian discus thrower and shot putter. Vikas Gowda won his first gold medal in Asian Championship in 2013 in Pune. His best moment came when he clinched the Gold for India in the 2014 Commonwealth games. He has qualified for the 2016 Olympics in the discus throw.

content:
Which of the following statement(s) regarding recent developments in international affairs is/are correct?

1. NATO's Defence Ministers agreed to boost support for Ukraine with a Comprehensive Package of Assistance, which will help it strengthen defence and to modernise its armed forces.

2. Israel announces plans for construction of dwelling units for Jewish settlers in a Palestinian neighbourhood of East Jerusalem.

Select the correct answer using the codes given below.
explanation:
NATO's Defence Ministers agreed to support for Ukraine with a Comprehensive Package of Assistance. The package aims :

$$\bullet$$ To help Ukraine strengthen its defences by building stronger security structures.

$$\bullet$$ To help Ukraine establish more effective and efficient defence and security structures, and to strengthen civilian control over them.

$$\bullet$$ To develop new projects, including in the areas of countering hybrid warfare and explosive devices.

Israel authorities have approved plans for the construction of 800 new housing units for Jewish settlers in a Palestinian neighbourhood of East Jerusalem. Hence both statements are correct.

content: Which country won the Thomas Cup Badminton Championship, 2016?
explanation: The Thomas Cup, sometimes called the World Men's Team Championship, is an international badminton competition among teams representing member nations of the Badminton World Federation (BWF). Thomas Cup Badminton Championship, 2016 won by Denmark. Thomas Cup after defeating Indonesia in the 2016 final. It was held in China. Thomas Cup 2018 was held in Bangkok (Thailand) and won by China. Thomas Cup 2020, 2022 and 2024 will be hosted by Denmark, Thailand and China respectively.

explanation: The world's largest biomass cookstove system for cooking in community kitchen has been installed at Shirdi in Maharashtra to cook for 20,000 people per day and is saving around 60,000 kg of LPG every year. The kitchen is completely based on solar cooking system for community kitchen.

options.content: International Day against Drug Abuse and Illicit Trafficking

explanation: International Yoga Day or Yoga Day, is celebrated on 21st June and was declared so by United Nations General Assembly (UNGA) on 11th December, 2014. Yoga is a physical, mental and spiritual practice or discipline that originated in India.

explanation: In a historic event in Telangana for the first time women combat pilots were induced in the Indian Air Force. The then Defence Minister Manohar Parrikar Commissioned three fighter pilots Mohana Singh, Bhawana Kanth and Avani Chaturvedi. explanation: Nabhah Sprsam Diptam (Touch the sky with glory) is the motto of Indian Air Force. This has been taken from eleventh chapter of the Gita, the discourse given by Lord Krishna to Arjuna during the war of Mahabharata.

explanation: The theme of 2017 World Health Day campaign was Depression : Let's Talk. 'World Health Day', celebrated on 7th April every year to mark the anniversary of the founding of WHO. explanation: India celebrated 100 years of Gandhiji's Satyagraha in Champaran in April, 2017. The Champaran Satyagraha of 1917 was the first Satyagraha Movement inspired by Gandhi and a major revolt bin the Indian Independence Movement. It was farmer's uprising that took place in Champaran.

explanation: On 18th April, 2017, whole world celebrated 'World Heritage Day'. Its theme was 'Cultural Heritage Sustainable Tourism'. The theme of World Heritage Day, 2019 was Rural Landscapes.

explanation: The USA dropped the 'Mother of All Bombs', the most powerful conventional bomb in the American arsenal, on an Islamic State cave complex in Afghanistan. The strike was the first combat use of what is formally named the GBU-43/B Massive Ordinance Air Blast.

content: Who among the following is the winner of the Singapore Open Super Series Badminton Men's Singles title, 2017?
explanation: The 2017 Singapore Open Super Series was the fourth Super Series Tournament of the 2017 BWF Super Series. The tournament takes place in Singapore City, (Singapore) from 11-16th April, 2017. B Sai Praneeth won the Singapore Open Super Series, 2017. Kento Momoto won the Singapore Open Super Series, 2019.

content: Which one of the following agencies enforces the laws on food security in India?
explanation: Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) is an autonomous body established under the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, Government of India. It is responsible for protecting and promoting public health through supervision of food safety.

content: Who among the following personalities is not a Governor of any Indian State?
explanation: Except Arvind Subramanian, all other given personalities are or remained governor of any Indian State. Arvind Subramanian is an Indian economist and the former Chief Economic Advisor to the Government of India, who served the position from 16th October, 2014 to 20th June 2018.

content: Which of the following cricket teams was defeated by India to lift the Women's T-20 Asia Cup, 2016?
explanation:
Indian women's squad beat Pakistan by 17 runs in the final match of Women's T-20 Asia Cup, 2016 in Bangkok. Mithali Raj was player of the match as well as player of the series.

In Women's T-20 Asia Cup, 2018, Bangladesh defeated India by 3 wickets.

content: Which one of the following was the venue of second BRICS Youth Summit of the Ministers, Officials and Youth Delegations?
explanation: The second BRICS Youth Summit was held in Guwahati, Assam with the theme 'Youth as bridge for intra-BRICS exchanges'.

content: Headquarters of the World Meteorological Organisation is located in
explanation: World Meteorological Organisation (WMO) is an inter-governmental organisation with a membership of 191 member states and territories. Its headquarter is in Geneva. It is originated from the International Meteorological Organisation (IMO). It is a specialised agency of the United Nations for meteorology.

content: Which one of the following countries is the largest trading partner of India in external trade for the year 2015-16?
explanation: China was the largest trade partner of India in its external trade for the financial year 2015-16. The cumulative trading amount was 65 billion USD including 56.95 billion USD worth imports and exports worth 8.25 billion USD. For the year 2018-19 also China is the largest trading partner of India.

content: Which one of the following cities hosted the 3rd World Trauma Congress?
explanation: New Delhi hosted the 3rd World Trauma Congress with the purpose of exchange ideas, experiences and lessons learned about the role of internationally accepted systems and protocols. 5th World Trauma Congress (2020) to be held in Australia.

content:

1. United States, 2. China, 3. Great Britain, 4. Russia

Select the correct answer using the codes given below.
explanation: Rio Olympics, 2016 was an international multi-sport event that was held in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil. The United States topped the medal tally by winning highest number of gold (46) and overall medals (121). USA is followed by Great Britain (67), China (70) and Russia (56). So, on the basis of medals earned the correct ascending order of countries will be Russia < Great Britain < China < USA. Hence, option (c) is the correct choice. Rio Olympic, 2020 to be held in Tokyo, Japan.

content: Which one of the following is the official Mascot of the FIFA World Cup, 2018?
explanation: The official mascot for the FIFA World Cup, 2018 tournament was Zabivaka - A wolf dressed in the colours of the Russian national team. The 2018 FIFA World Cup was the 21st FIFA World Cup, an international football tournament contested by the men's national teams of the member associations of FIFA, once every four years. France became the champion of FIFA World Cup, 2018. FIFA 2022 will be scheduled in Qatar.

content: The Headquarters of the proposed National Sports University (as per the National Sports University Ordinance, 2018) will be set-up in
explanation: National Sports University is a sports university proposed to be set-up with its headquarters at Manipur in India. It was proposed to be set-up by the National Sports University Ordinance, 2018. The Ordinance was designed on the lines of the National Sports University Bill, 2017.

content: India, in June, 2018, asserted that any mega connectivity project must respect sovereignty and territorial integrity of the countries. The project referred to above is
explanation: The Belt and Road Initiative (BRI), also known as One Belt One Road (OBOR) initiative is a Chinese effort which focusses on improving connectivity and cooperation among Asian countries, Africa, China and Europe. With the exception of Bhutan, which has no diplomatic ties with China, almost every other South Asian country has signed into OBOR, but India has not signed it. The main reason behind India's opposition towards the policy is China-Pakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC), which is a part of OBOR. Keeping this in mind, in 2018 Indian asserted that to be a part of any mega project, it is necessary that the project must respect sovereignty and territorial must respect sovereignty and territorial integrity of the countries.

content: Who among the following is not a member of G-7?
explanation: Russia is not a member of G-7. The Group of Seven (G-7) is a group of seven largest advanced economies in the world, consisting of Canada, France, Germany, Italy, Japan, the United Kingdom and the United States. The 44th edition of G-7 Summit, 2018 was hosted by Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau in Quebec (Canada) on 8-9th June. The 45th G-7 Summit, 2019 was held at France.

content: Dr. Bindeshwari Pathak, who was awarded Nikkei Asia Prize, 2018 for Culture and Community, is the founder of

options.content: Sulabh Sanitation and Social Reform Movement

explanation: Dr. Bindeshwari Pathak, a noted social reformer and founder of Sulabh Sanitation and Social Reform Movement, was honoured with Japan's prestigious 'Nikkei Asia Prize for Culture and Community' for his significant work in tackling poor hygiene and discrimination. The award was launched in 1996, and awarded for significant contributions in one of the three areas - regional growth; science, technology and innovation; and culture and community. Earlier former Prime Minister Manmohan Singh and Infosys Chairman Narayan Murthy are among the few Indians who have won the prize.

content:
Which of the following statements about Indian Academy of Highway Engineers is/are correct?

1. it is a registered society.

2. It is a collaborative body of both Central Government and State Governments.

Select the correct answer using the codes given below.
explanation: Indian Academy of Highway Engineers (IAHE) is a registered society under the administrative control of Ministry of Road Transport and Highways, Government of India. IAHE is a collaborative body of the central and state governments. The primary objective of IAHE is to fulfill the long standing need for Training of Highway Engineers at the entry level and during the service. Hence, both statements 1 and 2 are correct.

content: Bharatmala Project is related to

options.content: interlinking major cities with gas pipelines

explanation: Bharatmala Project is the second largest highways construction project in the country. Bharatmala will look to improve road connectivity particularly on economic corridors, border areas and far flung areas with an aim of quicker movement of cargo and boosting exports. The ambitious umbrella programme is to subsume all existing highway projects including the flagship National Highways Development Project (NHDP).

content:

2. The festival was organized by the Ministry of Tourism, Government of India.

Select the correct answer using the codes given below.
explanation:
The Aadi Mahotsav, was held at Dilli Haat, New Delhi, on 30th November, 2017. The theme of this festival was 'A Celebration of the Spirit of Tribal Culture, Cuisine and Commerce'. Hence, statement (1) is correct. It was organized by the Tribal Secretary and not by Ministry of Tourism. Hence Statement (2) is incorrect.

The 2019 edition of the Aadi Mahotsav held at Leh in Ladakh.

content: Which of the following statements regarding construction of Rohtang Tunnel is not correct?

options.content: It is located at an altitude of 5,000 feet
It will provide all-year connectivity to Lahaul and Spiti Valley
The tunnel is being built by the Border Roads Organisation
It will reduce the length of the Leh-Manali Highway by approximately 50 km

explanation: Option (a) is not correct because the Rohtang Tunnel is located at an altitude of 3,100 metres (10,171 ft.). It is being built by the Border Roads Organisation to provide all year connectivity to Lahaul and Spiti Valley. It also aims at reducing the distance of Leh-Manali Highway by approx 50 km.

content: Who among the following recently became the first woman pilot in Indian Navy?
explanation: Subhangi Swaroop has made history by becoming the first woman to be induced into the Indian Navy as a pilot. Shubhangi Swaroop is belonged to Bareilly in Uttar Pradesh.

explanation: Italy has failed to qualify for the first FIFA World Cup in 60 years after play-off defeat to Sweden.

explanation:
Defence Technology and Trade Initiative (DTTI), originally called the 'Carter Initiative' after the name of US Deputy Secretary of Defence Ashton Carter.

Its main objective is to strengthen defence cooperation by facilitating the Indian companies to collaborate with US partners in defence co-production, where the US provides technology and guidance for building modern weapon systems.

content: As per the policy applicable in 2017, how much Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) is permitted in the defence sector in India?

options.content: 26 percent through the automatic route and beyond that up to 49 percent through the government route

explanation: In the defence sector, Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) up to 49% is permitted under the automatic route. Foreign Direct Investment beyond 49% and up to 100% is permitted through government approval, wherever it is likely to result in access to modern technology or for other reasons to be recorded.

content: Which one of the following countries did not participate in the 21st Edition of Exercise Malabar?
explanation:
The 2017 Malabar Exercise was the 21st Edition of the Exercise and conducted from 10th to 17th July, 2017. This edition involved navies from India, USA and Japan. The exercise included a harbor phase at Chennai. Hence, Australia was not a participant of this exercise.

Malabar Exercise, 2019 (23rd edition) was held in Japan from 26th September to 4th October, 2019.

explanation: Sir Christopher John Greenwood is a British judge at the International Court of Justice. He was elected on 6th November, 2008. He pulled out before the 12th round of voting.

content: In order to review the Income Tax Act, 1961 and to draft a new Direct Tax Law in consonance with economic needs of the country, the Government of India in November, 2017 has constituted a Task Force. Who among the following is made the convenor of it?
explanation: The Union Government has constituted a Task Force to review the Income Tax Act, 1961 and draft a new Direct Tax Law in consonance with current economic needs of the country. Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) member Arbind Modi will be the convenor of Task Force while Chief Economic Adviser Dr. Arvind Subramanian will be a permanent Special Invitee. The Task Force will draft an appropriate direct tax legislation keeping in view the direct tax system in various countries, international best practices and economic needs of the country. It will submit its report within six months.

content: The headquarters of National Research Laboratory for Conservation of Cultural Property (NRLC) is located at
explanation: The National Research Laboratory for Conservation of Cultural Property (NRLC) is a scientific institution of the Ministry of Culture, Government of India. It is responsible for the Conservation of Cultural Heritage. The NRLC was established in 1976 with its headquarters located at Lucknow.

explanation: The first Utkrisht Double Decker Air-Conditioned Yatri (UDAY) Express was a specially designed trains which started service between Coimbatore and Bengaluru in June, 2018. Second, such service was launched in September, 2019 that runs between Visakhapatnam and Vijayawada.

explanation: Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna Award is the highest sporting honour of India. It is annually awarded to players by the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports. Bajrang Punia (Wrestling) and Deepa Malik (Parathlete) were awarded with this award in 2019.

The Tirur Vettila (Betel leaf) from Kerala has got its GI tag. The leaf given its unique component i.e., Eugenol in leaves along with high protein and chlorophyll content has several medicinal properties.

explanation: The Ministry of Railways had launched the Commando for Railway Security (CORAS) of Indian Railways in August, 2019. explanation: World Humanitarian Day is an international day celebrated on 19th August every year to recognise the humanitarian personnel and those who laid down their lives for the causes of humanity. explanation:
National Water Academy (NWA), a centre of excellence in training and capacity building in water resource, is located in Pune (Maharashtra). It was set-up under Central Water Commission by the Ministry of Water Resources in 1988.

content: 'Mission Indradhanush' is related to
explanation: 'Mission Indradhanush' is related to full immunisation. This health mission was launched by the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare on 25th December, 2014. The scheme seeks to drive towards 90% full immunisation coverage of India and sustain same by year 2020. Vaccination is being provided against eight vaccine preventable diseases, i.e., Diphtheria, Pertussis, Tetanus, Polio, Measles, Tuberculosis, Hepatitis B, Meningitis, Rotavirus, Diarrhea and Japanese Encephalitis.

content: IMBEX is a joint exercise conducted regularly between Armies of India and
explanation:
India-Myanmar Bilateral Army Exercise (IMBEX) is a joint exercise conducted regularly between Armies of India and Myanmar.

This joint exercise is also the first of its kind military training exercise on United Nations Peacekeeping Operations between India and Myanmar.

content: Who among the following was awarded The Hindu Prize in Fiction category for the year 2018?

explanation: The 15th Pravasi Bhartiya Divas, 2019 was held in Varanasi (Uttar Pradesh) from 21st to 23rd January, 2019. Consider the following statements with regard to a steel plant:
I. It was setup in 1964 with Russian collaboration
II. It receives iron ore from the Rourkela region
III. Water and Hydel power is supplied by the Damodar Valley Corporation
Identify the Steel Plant on the basis of the above facts:
explanation:
The correct steel plant, based on the provided facts, is the Bhilai Steel Plant.

Key Points

Bhilai Steel Plant: Established in 1955 through collaboration with the Soviet Union, it is located in Chhattisgarh.

Iron Ore Supply: This plant sources its iron ore primarily from the Dalli-Rajhara mines, not the Rourkela region.

Product Specialization: It is renowned for producing rails for the Indian Railways along with a variety of steel products.

Energy Supply: The Damodar Valley Corporation provides water and hydel power to the plant.

Impact: Bhilai Steel Plant has significantly contributed to making India a self-reliant producer of steel and played a pivotal role in the country's industrial advancement.

Additional Information

Damodar Valley Corporation (DVC):

A government entity established in 1948.

Provides hydel and thermal power to eastern Indian industries and municipalities.

Manages multiple dams, reservoirs, and power plants in the Damodar River basin.

Steel Plants in India:

Major steel plants include Bhilai, Bokaro, Rourkela, and Durgapur.

Each plant is known for distinctive collaborations and specific sources for raw materials and energy.

Russian Collaboration in Indian Steel Industry:

The Soviet Union significantly contributed to India's steel industry, including the Bhilai and Bokaro plants.

Marked a landmark in Indo-Soviet relations during the mid-20th century.

Indian Steel Industry Overview:

India stands as one of the largest steel producers globally, with a comprehensive network of integrated and mini steel plants.

The industry is vital for infrastructure development and economic growth.

content: Gaja Dwar, Ashwa Dwar, Garuda Dwar, Makar Dwar, Shardula Dwar and Hamsa Dwar are located in a building at
explanation:
The monumental gates Gaja Dwar, Ashwa Dwar, Garuda Dwar, Makar Dwar, Shardula Dwar, and Hamsa Dwar are located at the Rashtrapati Bhavan in New Delhi.

Key Points

Monumental Gates: These gates are significant entrances to the Rashtrapati Bhavan, the official residence of the President of India, and are architecturally significant, representing different symbolic animals.

Architectural Heritage: Designed by Sir Edwin Lutyens and Herbert Baker, Rashtrapati Bhavan is an icon of British colonial architecture, inaugurated in 1931. The gates symbolize the heritage and cultural motifs of India.

Ceremonial Function: Besides their architectural beauty, these gates serve ceremonial purposes, underlining the historical importance of Rashtrapati Bhavan.

Additional Information

Rashtrapati Bhavan:

Scale and Function: It spans 130 hectares, making it one of the largest residences of a head of state in the world, containing 340 rooms, including official spaces for the President, guest accommodations, and ceremonial areas.

Mughal Gardens: Known for their beauty, these gardens are open to the public during the annual "Udyanotsav."

Architectural Style:

Design: The building combines classical European and Indian architectural styles. The dome is inspired by the Sanchi Stupa, with elements reflecting Indian traditions.

Ceremonial Gates:

Symbolism: Each gate, named after an animal, embodies qualities such as strength and wisdom, integral to Indian culture. The gates are adorned with intricate motifs and carvings enhancing their aesthetic value.

Sir Edwin Lutyens:

Legacy: As the chief architect of New Delhi, Lutyens designed numerous landmark structures, including the Rashtrapati Bhavan. The term "Lutyens' Delhi" denotes the area showcasing his architectural influence.

content: Which dance form of India has been inscribed into UNESCO’s Representative List of Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity?
explanation:
The correct answer is Garba.

Key Points

Garba, a traditional dance from Gujarat, India, was added to UNESCO's Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity in 2022.

It is primarily performed during the Navratri festival, which honors Goddess Durga and celebrates the triumph of good over evil.

The dance symbolizes devotion, community bonding, and festivity, typically performed in circles around a lamp or an image of the goddess.

Garba's recognition by UNESCO underscores its cultural importance and helps in preserving and promoting India's intangible heritage worldwide.

Additional Information

UNESCO Representative List of Intangible Cultural Heritage:

The purpose of this list is to protect cultural traditions and practices that communities consider part of their heritage.

India has several elements on this list, including Yoga, Kumbh Mela, and Garba.

Garba vs. Dandiya Raas:

Garba uses hand and foot movements, whereas Dandiya incorporates sticks.

Both are popular during Navratri but vary in technique and presentation.

Navratri Festival:

A nine-day festival dedicated to Goddess Durga, celebrated with great enthusiasm across India, particularly in Gujarat.

Garba is a key component of Navratri festivities.

India and Intangible Cultural Heritage:

India boasts a rich tradition of diverse art forms, practices, and festivals recognized globally.

There are ongoing efforts to document and preserve these traditions for future generations.

content: India’s Gas Turbine Research Establishment (GTRE), part of the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) has developed an aero-engine which will potentially make India self-reliance in aero-engine technology. What is the name of the engine?
explanation:
The correct answer is Kaveri.

Key Points

The Kaveri engine is an indigenous aero-engine developed by the Gas Turbine Research Establishment (GTRE), a laboratory under the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO).

It was specifically designed for India's Light Combat Aircraft (LCA) Tejas to achieve self-reliance in aviation propulsion systems.

The Kaveri engine project was initiated in the 1980s as part of India's efforts to reduce dependence on foreign suppliers for military equipment.

Although the Kaveri engine faced delays and challenges, its development has significantly contributed to India's expertise and technological capabilities in aero-engine manufacturing.

The engine has a thrust rating of approximately 80 kilonewtons, suitable for lightweight fighter jets like the LCA Tejas.

Additional Information

Gas Turbine Research Establishment (GTRE)

GTRE, established in 1959, is a DRDO laboratory specializing in gas turbine engine development for military and civil applications.

It plays a pivotal role in designing and testing advanced propulsion systems for India's aerospace sector.

Challenges in Aero-Engine Development

Aero-engine development involves complex requirements such as advanced metallurgy, thermal management, and precision engineering.

India faced challenges in sourcing advanced materials and overcoming technological gaps during the Kaveri project.

Light Combat Aircraft (LCA) Tejas

The Tejas is a multi-role lightweight fighter aircraft developed by Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) for the Indian Air Force and Navy.

While initially intended to be powered by the Kaveri engine, the Tejas was equipped with the GE F404 engine due to delays in the Kaveri program.

Self-Reliance in Defence

The Kaveri engine project aligns with India's broader goal of achieving self-reliance in defence technology, complementing initiatives like "Make in India."

Despite the delays, the program has enriched India's knowledge base and will be valuable for future indigenous engine development.

content: Which among the following agencies releases report on ‘S.A.F.E. Accommodation: Worker Housing for Manufacturing Growth’?

Purpose of the Report: The report focuses on delivering safe and affordable housing for industrial and manufacturing workers. This aligns with government efforts to enhance worker welfare and stimulate economic growth by improving living standards in urban and industrial settings.

HUDCO's Contributions: HUDCO has been involved in various studies and reports aimed at tackling housing challenges and advancing urban infrastructure development in India.

Additional Information

HUDCO Overview:

Founded in 1970, HUDCO specializes in financing housing and urban infrastructure projects across India.

It operates under the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs.

HUDCO also offers consultancy services for sustainable urban development projects.

Worker Housing Challenges:

The absence of affordable and safe housing negatively affects productivity and well-being.

Government-supported initiatives, such as HUDCO's report, aim to systematically address these issues.

Affordable Housing Schemes in India:

State governments implement specific housing schemes that address local needs.

HUDCO collaborates with these schemes to ensure effective implementation and funding.

Sustainable Urban Development:

HUDCO encourages the use of green building technologies and eco-friendly construction practices.

The organization is committed to inclusive growth by addressing the housing and infrastructure needs of marginalized communities.

HUDCO's initiatives contribute to India's commitment to achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

content:
Consider the following statements:

2. Garba of Gujarat has been declared by UNESCO as an intangible cultural heritage

explanation:
Kozhikode has been included in UNESCO's creative cities network as the 'City of Music'

This statement is incorrect. Kozhikode (also known as Calicut) was indeed included in UNESCO's Creative Cities Network, but it was designated as the 'City of Literature', not the 'City of Music'. The 'City of Music' designation went to Gwalior.

Garba of Gujarat has been declared by UNESCO as an intangible cultural heritage

This statement is correct. The traditional dance form of Garba from Gujarat was officially inscribed on UNESCO's Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity in December 2023.

Gwalior has been included in UNESCO's creative cities network as the 'City of Literature'

This statement is incorrect. Gwalior was also included in UNESCO's Creative Cities Network, but it received the designation of 'City of Music', not the 'City of Literature'. As mentioned above, Kozhikode was named the 'City of Literature'.

So, only the second statement is correct.

Therefore, the answer is 1.

So, out of the three statements, only one is correct.

Consider the following UN Declarations/Covenants :

1. Universal Declaration of Human Rights

2. Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination Against Women

3. International Covenant of Economic, Social and Cultural Rights

4. Convention on Refugees

Which one of the following is the correct chronological order (starting with the earliest) of the above ?
explanation:
To determine the correct chronological order of the given UN Declarations/Covenants, we need to know the specific years in which each was adopted :

1. Universal Declaration of Human Rights (UDHR) was adopted on December 10, 1948.

2. Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination Against Women (CEDAW) was adopted on December 18, 1979.

3. International Covenant of Economic, Social and Cultural Rights (ICESCR) was adopted on December 16, 1966.

4. Convention on Refugees (Refugee Convention) was adopted on July 28, 1951.

Now, let's list these years in order to determine the correct sequence :

- 1948 : Universal Declaration of Human Rights (1)

- 1951 : Convention on Refugees (4)

- 1966 : International Covenant of Economic, Social and Cultural Rights (3)

- 1979 : Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination Against Women (2)

The correct chronological order is :

1. Universal Declaration of Human Rights

2. Convention on Refugees

3. International Covenant of Economic, Social and Cultural Rights

4. Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination Against Women

Therefore, the correct option is :

Option A : 1, 4, 3, 2

2. The Government of India has accorded its approval for India's participation in the international mega science project.

Select the answer using the code given below :
explanation:
The Square Kilometre Array (SKA) project is indeed a significant international endeavor aimed at creating the world's largest and most sensitive radio telescope. Let’s evaluate the given statements :

Statement 1 : SKA is a state-of-the-art, mega science international facility to build the world's biggest and most sensitive radio telescope for addressing a wide variety of cutting-edge science goals.

This statement is correct. The SKA project aims to construct an extensive array of radio telescopes with an effective collecting area of one square kilometer. This facility will enable unprecedented precision in radio astronomy and will help answer fundamental questions about the universe, such as the formation of galaxies, the nature of dark matter, and the potential for life beyond Earth.

Statement 2 : The Government of India has accorded its approval for India's participation in the international mega science project.

This statement is also correct. The Government of India has indeed approved participation in the SKA project, highlighting India's commitment to contributing to and benefiting from this cutting-edge international scientific endeavor.

Therefore, both statements 1 and 2 are correct.

Select the answer using the code given below :

Option C : Both 1 and 2

content: Who among the following is known as the architect of Indian planning?
explanation:
The correct answer is Option C, P.C. Mahalanobis.

P.C. Mahalanobis is widely recognized as the architect of Indian planning due to his pivotal role in shaping the country's economic policies after independence. He is best known for introducing the Mahalanobis model of economic development, which heavily emphasized the development of heavy industries and a focus on self-reliance.

Here's why the other options are incorrect:

Option A, V.K.R.V. Rao: While V.K.R.V. Rao was a renowned economist and played a significant role in Indian economic planning, he was more focused on agricultural development and social welfare aspects. He is considered a champion of rural development and was instrumental in shaping India's Five-Year Plans.

Option B, Sukhamoy Chakravarty: Sukhamoy Chakravarty was a prominent economist who later served as the chairman of the Planning Commission. However, he primarily contributed to the theoretical and mathematical aspects of economic planning, rather than being the architect of the overall framework.

Option D, John Mathai: John Mathai was the first Finance Minister of independent India. While he played a role in the initial stages of economic planning, his contributions were more focused on financial management and policy implementation.

In conclusion, P.C. Mahalanobis's significant contributions, particularly the Mahalanobis model, established him as the architect of Indian planning, guiding the nation's economic development strategy for several decades after independence.

content:
Which among the following statements regarding the election of non-permanent members to the United Nations Security Council is/are correct?

2. Six members are elected each year by the General Assembly for two year term

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
explanation:
Let's analyze each statement regarding the election of non-permanent members to the United Nations Security Council:

Statement 1: "Five members coming from Africa and Asia, one from Eastern Europe, two from Latin America and two from Western Europe and other states"

This statement is correct. The allocation of the ten non-permanent seats on the United Nations Security Council is distributed among various regional groups as follows:

  • 5 seats for African and Asian States
  • 1 seat for Eastern European States
  • 2 seats for Latin American and Caribbean States
  • 2 seats for Western European and other States

Statement 2: "Six members are elected each year by the General Assembly for a two-year term"

This statement is incorrect. The General Assembly elects five non-permanent members each year for a two-year term. This ensures a rotational system where five new members join the Security Council each year, resulting in overlapping terms.

Given our analysis of each statement, the correct answer is:

Option A: 1 only

content:
Consider the following statements about Centralized Laboratory Net- work (CLN):

1. CLN works to test vaccines can be used during which pandemic

2. India is a member of CLN

3. CLN is a part of the Coalition for Epidemic Preparedness Innovations (CEPI)

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation:
The correct answer is Option D: 1, 2 and 3. Here's why:

1. CLN works to test vaccines can be used during which pandemic

This statement is correct. The Centralized Laboratory Network (CLN) is a global network of laboratories established to accelerate the development and testing of vaccines during a pandemic. Its main purpose is to provide a centralized platform for coordinating and standardizing vaccine testing procedures, ensuring quicker and more efficient vaccine development.

2. India is a member of CLN

This statement is also correct. India is an active member of the Centralized Laboratory Network (CLN). The Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR) is a key participant in the CLN, contributing its expertise and resources to the network's activities.

3. CLN is a part of the Coalition for Epidemic Preparedness Innovations (CEPI)

This statement is correct. The Centralized Laboratory Network (CLN) is indeed a part of the Coalition for Epidemic Preparedness Innovations (CEPI). CEPI is a global partnership that works to accelerate the development of vaccines against emerging infectious diseases. CLN serves as a critical component within CEPI's overall strategy for pandemic preparedness.

In summary, all three statements accurately describe the Centralized Laboratory Network (CLN) and its role in global pandemic preparedness.

content:
Consider the following statements about Kavach, the automatic train protection system:

1. It is designed and developed by Research Design and Standard Organisation (RDSO) in collaboration with Railway Technical Research Institute (RTRI), Japan

2. The trials of Kavach were facilitated by the Southern Railway

3. Kavach is a state-of-the-art electronic system with Safety Integrity Level-4 (SIL-4)

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
explanation:
Let's analyze each of the statements about Kavach, the automatic train protection system:

1. It is designed and developed by Research Design and Standard Organisation (RDSO) in collaboration with Railway Technical Research Institute (RTRI), Japan

This statement is incorrect. The Kavach system was designed and developed indigenously by the Research Design and Standard Organisation (RDSO) in India in collaboration with multiple local firms and not specifically with the Railway Technical Research Institute (RTRI) of Japan.

2. The trials of Kavach were facilitated by the Southern Railway

This statement is also incorrect. The trials of Kavach were facilitated primarily by the South Central Railway (SCR) zone, not the Southern Railway.

3. Kavach is a state-of-the-art electronic system with Safety Integrity Level-4 (SIL-4)

This statement is correct. Kavach is indeed a state-of-the-art electronic system that has Safety Integrity Level-4 (SIL-4), which is the highest level of safety integrity.

Therefore, only statement 3 is correct.

The correct option is:

Option B: 3 only

content: The UN COP-27 Summit relates to:
explanation:
The correct answer is Option C: Climate change.

Here's why:

The UN COP-27 Summit stands for the 27th Conference of the Parties of the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change. It's an annual event where world leaders, scientists, activists, and other stakeholders gather to discuss and negotiate global action on climate change. The primary focus of these conferences is:

  • Assessing the global climate situation: The summit reviews the current state of the climate, analyzes data, and evaluates the progress made in combating climate change.
  • Setting targets and commitments: Countries commit to specific goals and actions to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and adapt to the impacts of climate change.
  • Developing solutions: COP summits facilitate the development of new technologies, policies, and financial mechanisms to address climate change.

The other options are unrelated to the COP-27 Summit:

  • Option A: Russia-Ukraine war: This is a geopolitical conflict with no direct connection to the COP-27 Summit.
  • Option B: Terrorism and counter-terrorism: While climate change can contribute to instability and conflict, the COP-27 Summit is not specifically focused on terrorism.
  • Option D: Interpol: Interpol is an international law enforcement agency and is not involved in the COP-27 Summit.

content: Which of the following Indian Naval Ships were decommissioned in June 2022?
explanation:
The correct answer is INS Nishank and INS Akshay.

  • The Indian Navy bid farewell to its two ships, INS Nishank and INS Akshay, at Naval Dockyard in Mumbai on 3 June 2022.
  • Both ships were decommissioned at Naval Dockyard after 32 years of service.
  • Nishank, the fourth of the Veer-class missile corvettes, was an integral part of the "Killer Squadron", renowned for its heroics in the 1971 war.

Additional Information

  • INS Gomati was decommissioned at the Naval Dockyard in Mumbai on 28 May 2022.
  • INS Gomati derives her name from the river Gomti and was commissioned on 16 April 1988.
  • It was the oldest warrior of the Western Fleet at the time of its decommissioning.
  • The ship will be dismantled and transported to Lucknow, where it will be installed as the "Gomati Shaurya Smarak," a museum celebrating Gomati’s service career.
  • For its contribution to national maritime security, INS Gomati was twice awarded the coveted Unit Citation, once in 2007-08 and again in 2019-20.

It is equipped with advanced sensors, weapons systems, and communication capabilities, making it a valuable asset for the Indian Navy.

The purchase of these helicopters is part of India's ongoing efforts to modernize its naval capabilities and enhance its maritime security posture.

content: Which one of the following planets has the highest density?
explanation:
Option D: Earth

Earth has the highest density among the planets in our solar system. The average density of Earth is approximately $$5.52 \, \text{g/cm}^3$$. This high density is due to its metallic core and abundant silicate materials. To compare, here are the average densities of the other planets:

Mercury: $$5.43 \, \text{g/cm}^3$$

Venus: $$5.24 \, \text{g/cm}^3$$

Jupiter: $$1.33 \, \text{g/cm}^3$$

Jupiter, despite being the largest planet, has a much lower density because it is primarily composed of hydrogen and helium, making it a gas giant. Earth’s composition with heavier elements like iron gives it a greater density than these other planets.

content: Which one of the following is not a bio-mass energy source?
explanation:
The source that is not considered a biomass energy source is:

Option B: Nuclear reactor

Biomass energy sources are typically derived from organic materials. They commonly include plant-based materials like wood, agricultural residues, and gases produced from biological processes (such as gobar gas, also known as biogas). Coal, while also originally derived from biological materials, is considered a fossil fuel due to its ancient origin and different energy characteristics compared to typical biomass resources. In contrast, nuclear reactors generate energy from nuclear fission processes and are not related to biological matter.
